results.rendered_html: śobhā f. (ifc. f(ā). ) splendour, brilliance, lustre, beauty, grace, loveliness (kā śobhā with loc., ‘what beauty is there [in that]’ i.e. ‘it has no beauty’; śobhāṃ na-√kṛ, ‘to look bad or ugly’; ifc. often = ‘splendid’, ‘excellent’ e.g. śaurya-śobhā, ‘splendid heroism’; karma-śobhā, ‘a masterpiece’),  TS.  &c. &c.

results.rendered_html:
śobhā [śubh-a]
1 Light, lustre, brilliance, radiance.
2 (a) Splendour, beauty, elegance, grace, loveliness; vapurabhinavamasyāḥ puṣyati svāṃ na śobhāṃ  Ś. 1. 19 ;  Me. 52 , 59.  (b) Natural beauty, grandeur (as of a mountain); adriśobhā  R. 2. 27. 
3 An ornament, graceful expression; śobhaiva maṃdarakṣu bdhakṣubhitāṃbhodhivarṇanā  Śi. 2. 107. 
4 Turmeric.
5 A kind of pigment (= gorocanā q. v.)
Comp.
— aṃjanaḥ N. of a very useful tree.
